introSection:
Canyon Blaster is an Arrow Dynamics indoor roller coaster located at Circus Circus in the Adventuredome in Las Vegas, Nevada, USA. It features back-to-back vertical loops and a double corkscrew, and ends with a helix inside an artificial mountain. It is also proclaimed as the world's largest indoor double-loop, double-corkscrew coaster.

Opened in 1993, Canyon Blaster is the second Arrow Dynamics coaster to be built for an indoor amusement park. The first was Chicago Loop at Old Chicago in Bolingbrook, Illinois, which was later relocated to Canobie Lake Park in 1987.

The ride is one of the two roller coasters at the park, with the other being El Loco. Its track and supports were engineered by Ride Centerline.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
Canyon Blaster opened on August 23, 1993 along with Adventuredome.

In 2001, the trains were repainted in a dark blue and deep purple heliochrome paint. However, it was changed to white and green livery somewhere between 2010 and 2011.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: The loud sound coming from the lift hill can be heard throughout the entire park.

sections.title: Ride experience
sections.content: After exiting the station, the train makes a right turn, goes through a short tunnel and climb a tall chain lift hill. Upon reaching the top, riders make a left turn before dropping, reaching a max speed of. The train then heads into two vertical loops. Riders hit a right turn, followed by a double corkscrew. After this, the train passes through a keyhole in the canyon, leading to an enclosed helix. Riders move right turn, hitting the final brake run and returning to the station.

sections.title: Color scheme
sections.content: Canyon Blaster has a strawberry track and white supports.

sections.title: Trains
sections.content: Canyon Blaster features the same car design as the defunct Drachen Fire at Busch Gardens Williamsburg. When it originally opened, it had seven-car trains. As of right now, it has six-car trains.

sections.title: In media
sections.content:
* Baby Geniuses - part of the fictional "Joyworld" theme park, Sly starts the ride with two scientists on board and restraints still open. Both fall from the ride.


 * Criss Angel Mindfreak (Season 3 Episode "Rollercoaster") - a train "goes through" Criss' body while he stands on the track after the double corkscrew and Criss ends up in the front seat.

fullText:
Canyon Blaster is an Arrow Dynamics indoor roller coaster located at Circus Circus in the Adventuredome in Las Vegas, Nevada, USA. It features back-to-back vertical loops and a double corkscrew, and ends with a helix inside an artificial mountain. It is also proclaimed as the world's largest indoor double-loop, double-corkscrew coaster.

Opened in 1993, Canyon Blaster is the second Arrow Dynamics coaster to be built for an indoor amusement park. The first was Chicago Loop at Old Chicago in Bolingbrook, Illinois, which was later relocated to Canobie Lake Park in 1987.

The ride is one of the two roller coasters at the park, with the other being El Loco. Its track and supports were engineered by Ride Centerline.

Canyon Blaster opened on August 23, 1993 along with Adventuredome.

In 2001, the trains were repainted in a dark blue and deep purple heliochrome paint. However, it was changed to white and green livery somewhere between 2010 and 2011.

The loud sound coming from the lift hill can be heard throughout the entire park.

After exiting the station, the train makes a right turn, goes through a short tunnel and climb a tall chain lift hill. Upon reaching the top, riders make a left turn before dropping, reaching a max speed of. The train then heads into two vertical loops. Riders hit a right turn, followed by a double corkscrew. After this, the train passes through a keyhole in the canyon, leading to an enclosed helix. Riders move right turn, hitting the final brake run and returning to the station.

Canyon Blaster has a strawberry track and white supports.

Canyon Blaster features the same car design as the defunct Drachen Fire at Busch Gardens Williamsburg. When it originally opened, it had seven-car trains. As of right now, it has six-car trains.


 * Baby Geniuses - part of the fictional "Joyworld" theme park, Sly starts the ride with two scientists on board and restraints still open. Both fall from the ride.


 * Criss Angel Mindfreak (Season 3 Episode "Rollercoaster") - a train "goes through" Criss' body while he stands on the track after the double corkscrew and Criss ends up in the front seat.
